tp:simple-type.name: Unreferenced
tp:simple-type.tp:docstring: Not referenced by the interface, so no Rust definition is generated.

tp:docstring.p: Provides access to the media player's playlists.
Since D-Bus does not directly support enums, or a maybe type, they are described in this interface.

tp:enumvalue.tp:docstring: The playback will stop when there are no more tracks to play.
The current track will start again from the beginning once it has finished playing.
The playback loops through a list of tracks.

tp:struct.name: Playlist
tp:struct.tp:docstring: A data structure describing a playlist.

tp:member.name: Id
tp:member.tp:docstring: A unique identifier for the playlist.

tp:member.name: Name
tp:member.tp:docstring: The name of the playlist, typically given by the user.

tp:struct.name: Maybe_Playlist
tp:struct.tp:docstring: A data structure describing a playlist, or nothing.

tp:member.name: Valid
tp:member.tp:docstring: Whether this structure refers to a valid playlist.

tp:member.name: Playlist
tp:member.tp:docstring: The playlist, providing Valid is true.

tp:mapping.name: Metadata_Map
tp:mapping.tp:docstring: A mapping from metadata attribute names to values.

tp:docstring.p: It is up to the media player whether this completely replaces the current tracklist, or whether it is merely inserted into the tracklist and the first track starts.

arg.name: playlist_id
arg.tp:docstring: The id of the playlist to activate.

arg.name: index
arg.tp:docstring: The index of the first playlist to be fetched (according to the ordering).

arg.name: max_count
arg.tp:docstring: The maximum number of playlists to fetch.

arg.name: order
arg.tp:docstring: The ordering that should be used.

arg.name: reverse_order
arg.tp:docstring: Whether the order should be reversed.

arg.name: playlists
arg.tp:docstring: A list of (at most max_count) playlists.

method.name: GetMetadata
method.tp:docstring: Gets the metadata of a playlist.

arg.name: mismatch
arg.tp:docstring: A tp:type not matching the signature falls back to the plain type.

tp:docstring.p: Indicates that either the Name or Icon attribute of a playlist has changed.
Client implementations should be aware that this signal may not be implemented.
tp:docstring.tp:rationale: Without this signal, media players have no way to notify clients of a change in the attributes of a playlist.

arg.name: playlist
arg.tp:docstring: The playlist which details have changed.

tp:docstring.p: The available orderings. At least one must be offered.
tp:docstring.tp:rationale: Media players may not have access to all the data required for some orderings.

tp:docstring.p: If there is no currently-active playlist, the structure's Valid field will be false, and the Playlist details are undefined.
